name: Columnar Scan Optimization
description: Split a wide table into narrow projection and extended attributes to optimize columnar scans

checks.name: events_core has 50000 rows
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM events_core;
checks.description: All 50,000 events must be in the core table

checks.name: events_core has 5 columns
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'events_core';
checks.description: events_core should have exactly 5 columns: event_id, event_date, user_id, event_type, amount

checks.name: events_extended has 50000 rows
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM events_extended;
checks.description: All 50,000 events must be in the extended table

checks.name: Join produces 50000 full rows
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM events_core c JOIN events_extended e ON c.event_id = e.event_id;
checks.description: Joining core and extended tables must recover all rows without any loss
